name: Swampland de Sitter conjecture
summary: Conjectures that any consistent theory of quantum gravity respects |grad V| / V >= c (with c an O(1) positive constant) for all scalar potentials. This bound forbids stable de Sitter vacua, implying that the observed cosmic acceleration cannot be a true cosmological constant but must be driven by a slowly-rolling scalar (quintessence) within string theory's allowed 'landscape'.

addresses_puzzles.note: Re-frames the problem: stable dS may not exist in the string landscape, so the question becomes 'how small can a slow-roll dark-energy field's V'/V be'. Compatible with quintessence models; in tension with Standard Model + cosmological constant being a consistent UV-complete theory.

touches_benchmarks.note: Predicts time-varying w(z) consistent with dynamical dark energy; DESI Y1 BAO + SN combinations weakly favor evolving DE in line with this picture.

introduces.note: Conjecture parameter c (O(1)) and the slow-roll potential of the quintessence field. The conjecture is a UV-completion-level constraint, not a model-builder's free parameter.

history: Obied, Ooguri, Spodyneiko, Vafa (2018), 'De Sitter Space and the Swampland', arXiv:1806.08362. Refined-dS conjecture (Ooguri-Palti-Shiu-Vafa 2018, arXiv:1810.05506) sharpens the formulation. Active and contested research direction in string phenomenology and cosmology.

references.citation: Obied, Ooguri, Spodyneiko, Vafa, 'De Sitter Space and the Swampland' (2018)
Ooguri, Palti, Shiu, Vafa, 'Distance and de Sitter Conjectures on the Swampland', Phys. Lett. B 788, 180 (2019)
